# REQ-FR-260022: Blank-First Workspace Entry with Explicit Search Activation

- Class: FR
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-05-08
- Source decision: Workspace entry model update (2026-05-08)

## Requirement

The frontend shall render the workspace blank immediately after sign-in, with no atoms or bonds shown until the user performs an explicit search action.

## Rationale

Blank-first entry makes the user's first action intentional, avoids implying that a default query has already been applied, and keeps workspace contents aligned with explicit search scope.

## Scope Notes

- In scope for this requirement: blank initial graph state after sign-in, no default atom preload, explicit user-triggered search as the first graph-population action.
- Out of scope for this requirement: the detailed search interaction design, label suggestion behavior, autocomplete behavior, and result-ranking rules.

## Verification

- Runtime check confirms the workspace graph is blank immediately after sign-in before any search is submitted.
- End-to-end flow confirms atoms appear only after an explicit search action.
- Regression checks confirm sign-in still lands in the workspace shell without crashing or preloading graph data.

## Traceability

- Related ADRs: ADR-260023
- Related backlog items: BI-260020
